title: A Giant Cosmic Sheet Around Our Galaxy
summary: Astronomers have found a giant flat sheet of matter around the Milky Way and Andromeda galaxies. This sheet is made of both normal matter and dark matter, which we cannot see. It stretches for tens of millions of light-years. The sheet helps explain why many nearby galaxies are moving away from us instead of being pulled in by gravity. Scientists used computer simulations to create a virtual twin of our cosmic neighborhood. They started with conditions from the early universe and evolved it to match today. The simulations show that the sheet's mass balances the gravity of the Local Group, which includes the Milky Way and Andromeda. This solves a mystery that puzzled astronomers for about fifty years. The discovery is exciting because it uses galaxy motions to understand mass distribution.

questions.question: How long has this mystery puzzled astronomers?

background_read: The universe is expanding, with most galaxies moving away from each other. Edwin Hubble discovered this in the 1920s, leading to the Big Bang theory. However, some galaxies like Andromeda are moving toward us. For decades, astronomers wondered why nearby galaxies move away despite gravity. The Local Group is a small cluster of galaxies, including the Milky Way and Andromeda, that should attract others. Dark matter is an invisible substance that makes up much of the universe's mass. Computer simulations help scientists test ideas by creating virtual models of space.
Article_Structure: WHO: Astronomers led by Ewoud Wempe
WHAT: Discovered a giant cosmic sheet of matter around the Milky Way
WHERE: Around the Local Group in space
WHY: To explain why nearby galaxies move away instead of being pulled by gravity

perspectives.description: The study uses simulations to solve a long-standing puzzle about galaxy motions.
